title: Intense heat continued to affect Punjab and Haryana on Thursday, with high temperatures persisting across both states for the past few days. Faridkot and Sirsa were the hottest places, officials said. - News Arena India
full_text:
Faridkot in Punjab recorded a maximum temperature of 45.9 degrees Celsius. Sirsa in Haryana recorded 45.8 degrees Celsius, as per the Meteorological Department. Many other parts of both states also faced severe heatwave conditions. Temperatures remained well above normal levels across most regions.
News Arena Network - Chandigarh - UPDATED: May 21, 2026, 07:21 PM - 2 min read
Representational image.
Intense heat continued to affect Punjab and Haryana on Thursday, with high temperatures persisting across both states for the past few days. Faridkot and Sirsa were the hottest places, officials said.
Faridkot in Punjab recorded a maximum temperature of 45.9 degrees Celsius. Sirsa in Haryana recorded 45.8 degrees Celsius, as per the Meteorological Department. Many other parts of both states also faced severe heatwave conditions. Temperatures remained well above normal levels across most regions.
Chandigarh, the common capital of Punjab and Haryana, also faced a hot day. The city recorded 44.2 degrees Celsius, which is more than four degrees above normal. In Haryana, Rohtak recorded a maximum temperature of 45.5 degrees Celsius. This was about five degrees above normal.
Ambala recorded 44.2 degrees Celsius, which is five degrees above normal. Hisar recorded 44.3 degrees Celsius, about three degrees above normal.
Karnal recorded a high of 43.8 degrees Celsius, around five degrees above normal. Bhiwani also faced a hot day with 44 degrees Celsius. Gurugram recorded a maximum of 42 degrees Celsius.
In Punjab, Bathinda also saw intense heatwave conditions. The city recorded a high of 45.6 degrees Celsius. Patiala recorded 44.9 degrees Celsius, which is five degrees above normal. Ludhiana recorded 43 degrees Celsius, around four degrees above normal. Amritsar recorded a maximum of 41.5 degrees Celsius.

title: Heatwave Grips Punjab and Haryana Faridkot and Sirsa Record Temperatures Above 45 Degrees - Rozana Spokesman
full_text:
Faridkot in Punjab and Sirsa in Haryana emerged as the hottest locations on Thursday as intense heatwave conditions continued to grip both states, pushing daytime temperatures well above seasonal averages and triggering widespread heat stress across the region.
Faridkot recorded a maximum temperature of 45.9 degrees Celsius, while Sirsa registered 45.8 degrees Celsius. These readings placed both districts at the top of the temperature chart in Punjab and Haryana amid persistent dry and hot weather conditions.
In Haryana, Rohtak recorded 45.5 degrees Celsius, around five degrees above normal, reflecting the severity of the ongoing heatwave. Other districts including Hisar, Bhiwani, Ambala, Karnal and Gurugram also remained under intense heat, with temperatures ranging between 42 and 44.3 degrees Celsius.
Chandigarh, the joint capital of the two states, reported a maximum temperature of 44.2 degrees Celsius, which was about four degrees higher than normal, indicating sustained heat pressure across the tricity region.
Punjab also witnessed widespread high temperatures. Bathinda touched 45.6 degrees Celsius, Patiala reached 44.9 degrees Celsius, Ludhiana recorded 43 degrees Celsius, and Amritsar registered 41.5 degrees Celsius, all reflecting above-normal conditions across major urban centres.
Meteorological observations indicated that heatwave conditions persisted across multiple pockets of Punjab and Haryana, with temperatures consistently staying above seasonal averages.
Authorities are continuing to monitor the situation as weather forecasts suggest that heatwave conditions may persist in parts of northern India in the coming days, keeping residents on alert amid rising health concerns linked to extreme temperatures.
